verses.text: David asks why the LORD stands far off and hides in times of trouble while the wicked proudly hunt the poor. The silence of God in suffering perplexes him. Faith may question God honestly, yet still address Him as Lord.

David asks why the LORD stands far off and hides in times of trouble while the wicked proudly hunt the poor. The silence of God in suffering perplexes him. Faith may question God honestly, yet still address Him as Lord.

The wicked boasts of his desires, blesses the greedy, and 'God is not in all his thoughts'; his ways prosper and he scorns his foes. Practical atheism marks him. To live as if God did not see is the root of all open evil.

The wicked boasts of his desires, blesses the greedy, and 'God is not in all his thoughts'; his ways prosper and he scorns his foes. Practical atheism marks him. To live as if God did not see is the root of all open evil.

The wicked boasts of his desires, blesses the greedy, and 'God is not in all his thoughts'; his ways prosper and he scorns his foes. Practical atheism marks him. To live as if God did not see is the root of all open evil.
He tells himself he shall never be moved, his mouth full of cursing and deceit, lurking to murder the innocent. He feels secure in his sin. The hardened heart mistakes God's patience for His absence.
He tells himself he shall never be moved, his mouth full of cursing and deceit, lurking to murder the innocent. He feels secure in his sin. The hardened heart mistakes God's patience for His absence.
He tells himself he shall never be moved, his mouth full of cursing and deceit, lurking to murder the innocent. He feels secure in his sin. The hardened heart mistakes God's patience for His absence.

Like a lion in his den he waits to seize the poor, saying in his heart, 'God hath forgotten: he hideth his face.' He counts on heaven's blindness. But the God he imagines forgetful sees every secret cruelty.

Like a lion in his den he waits to seize the poor, saying in his heart, 'God hath forgotten: he hideth his face.' He counts on heaven's blindness. But the God he imagines forgetful sees every secret cruelty.

Like a lion in his den he waits to seize the poor, saying in his heart, 'God hath forgotten: he hideth his face.' He counts on heaven's blindness. But the God he imagines forgetful sees every secret cruelty.

David cries, 'Arise, O LORD... lift up thine hand: forget not the humble,' asking why the wicked should despise God unpunished. He appeals to God to act. The oppressed find their strength in calling the Judge to remember.

David cries, 'Arise, O LORD... lift up thine hand: forget not the humble,' asking why the wicked should despise God unpunished. He appeals to God to act. The oppressed find their strength in calling the Judge to remember.

Yet God has seen it all, for the poor commits himself to Him, the helper of the fatherless; David asks Him to break the arm of the wicked. God is no idle spectator. He receives the trust of the helpless and will judge their oppressors.

Yet God has seen it all, for the poor commits himself to Him, the helper of the fatherless; David asks Him to break the arm of the wicked. God is no idle spectator. He receives the trust of the helpless and will judge their oppressors.

'The LORD is King for ever and ever'; He hears the desire of the humble and will judge for the fatherless and oppressed, that earthly man may no more oppress. The Psalm ends in confidence. The reign of God is the sure hope of all the downtrodden.
